references.title: Performance Prediction of Students in Higher Education Using Multi-Model Ensemble Approach

extracted.Students: 1201 BSc and MSc students from a Pakistani University
extracted.Data sources: Average Grade in Different Grading Elements
extracted.Features: [Assignment, Quizz, Presentation, Mid Term, Subjective, Objective, Previous Academics]
extracted.Preprocessing Details: For this research question, the authors start with selecting students from RQ1 from the departments that exhibit the highest dropout rates.
extracted.Models: Naive Bayes, J48, MLP, Bagging, Boosting, Stacking, Voting
extracted.Comments: Authors do multiple Research, with the first being the prediction of dropout.
extracted.Limitations:
- Small sample size

- Single institution Data

- No additional sources of data

extracted.Students: 1201 BSc and MSc students from a Pakistani University
extracted.Data sources: Average Grade in Different Grading Elements
extracted.Features: [Assignment, Quizz, Presentation, Mid Term, Subjective, Objective, Previous Academics]
extracted.Preprocessing Details: Grades C to F were considered Low Grades, while A and B were considered High Grades.
extracted.Models: Naive Bayes, J48, MLP, Bagging, Boosting, Stacking, Voting
extracted.Comments: The second question does a separation on Grade lines: A and B are High Grades and C to F is considered a Low Grade.
extracted.Limitations:
- Small sample size

- Single institution Data

- No additional sources of data

annotations.text: It is equally important and challenging to create early warning systems that are accurate and understandable. The paper look to use ML to create models for multiple different relevant tasks in LA:

The study focuses on comparing performance and efficiency of different ensemble classifiers for the aforementioned tasks. The auhtors rely on data extracted from a Pakistani university. Boosted Trees outperforms bagging when dataset is larger, while stacking is better for smaller datasets.
